evidence: quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:238 defines the DeltaF2Input block for B_s mixing.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:239 sets the backend key to b_s.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:244 sets the down-sector generations to (1, 2).
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:645 stores the B_s decay constant used by the hadronic evaluator.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:893 defines the B_s NP-budget helper.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 includes B_s in the modern phenomenology system IDs.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:135 defines the modern B_s policy entry.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:657 defines the modern bridge B_s mixing evaluator.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:1346 routes system_id == B_s to that evaluator.
quarkConstraints/qcd_running.py:7 documents the Delta F=2 operator basis used by B_s.

status_history.reason: Initial PKA draft deposit for Delta m_s / B_s mixing.
Writer polish completed for WA batch wa_w6_beauty.

issues: CHK-1: TeX quotes HFLAV companion values x_s, chi_s, 1/Gamma_s, and DeltaGamma_s, but they are not represented in pdg_or_equivalent value blocks.
CHK-1: TeX quotes LHCb Run-2 dataset and Delta m_s values that are in pdg_or_equivalent.dominant_lhcb_input, but that value block lacks an explicit year field.
CHK-1: TeX quotes FLAG f_Bs sqrt(Bhat_Bs) and xi numerical inputs under auxiliary_theory_inputs rather than pdg_or_equivalent.
status_history.reason: Checker ca_w6_beauty found strict CHK-1 metadata gaps; see flavor_catalog/worklogs/checker/ca_w6_beauty.md.
Cycle-2 writer rework addressed ca_w6_beauty CHK-1 metadata-completeness findings.

issues: CHK-1: TeX still quotes FLAG f_Bs sqrt(Bhat_Bs) = 256.1 +/- 5.7 MeV and xi = 1.216 +/- 0.016 outside pdg_or_equivalent; WA-v2 added complete metadata under auxiliary_theory_inputs instead.
status_history.reason: Cycle-2 checker found a strict CHK-1 pdg_or_equivalent placement gap; see flavor_catalog/worklogs/checker/ca_w6_beauty_v2.md.

APPROVE-OVERRIDE per L001 / B021_B023 precedent: pdg_or_equivalent is for measured experimental observables. The FLAG 2024 lattice inputs f_Bs sqrt(Bhat_Bs) = 256.1 +/- 5.7 MeV and xi = 1.216 +/- 0.016 are theoretical normalization averages and correctly live under auxiliary_theory_inputs.flag_2024_bmixing with full strict metadata. The HFLAV Fall 2024 recommended Delta m_s = 17.766 +/- 0.006 ps^-1, the HFLAV companion quantities (x_s, chi_s, 1/Gamma_s, DeltaGamma_s), the HFLAV/PDG 2025 cross-check, and the dominant LHCb 2022 input are all already in pdg_or_equivalent with complete metadata. See flavor_catalog/signoff/by_process/B001_B003.md.

status_history.notes: All measured and quoted prediction value claims verified against fetched source pages or local source snapshots.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\Delta m_s\).  This entry covers the
\(B_s^0-\bar B_s^0\) mass splitting, equivalently the oscillation frequency
measured in time-dependent flavor-tagged \(B_s\) decays.  It is the magnitude
observable in the \(\Delta B=2\), \(b\leftrightarrow s\) neutral-meson mixing
system.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical equivalent value used here is the HFLAV Fall 2024 recommended
lifetime-and-oscillation average recorded in \texttt{B003.yaml} under
\texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent}, source key \texttt{hflav\_2024\_dms}, with local
snapshot \texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/B003/hflav\_2024\_dms.txt}:
\[
  \Delta m_s = 17.766\pm0.006~{\rm ps}^{-1}
\]
from CDF, LHCb, and CMS.  The same HFLAV page gives the derived companion
quantities \(x_s=26.94\pm0.10\) and
\(\chi_s=0.499314\pm0.000005\), using
\(1/\Gamma_s=1.516\pm0.006~{\rm ps}\),
\(\Delta\Gamma_s=+0.0781\pm0.0035~{\rm ps}^{-1}\), and no CP violation in
mixing.  The published-only HFLAV/PDG 2025 preparation page, source key
\texttt{hflav\_pdg2025\_dms}, gives the nearly identical
\(\Delta m_s=17.765\pm0.006~{\rm ps}^{-1}\), snapshotted at
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/B003/hflav\_pdg2025\_dms.txt}.
sections.relevance:
In anarchic Randall--Sundrum flavor, tree-level KK-gluon and electroweak
exchange generate \(\Delta B=2\) four-quark operators.  \(B_s\) mixing is the
down-sector \(s-b\) analogue of \(B_d\) mixing and complements the stronger
kaon constraints by testing a different localization and CKM structure.  The
existing code treats \(\Delta m_s\) as a direct bound on the new-physics
contribution to \(M_{12}^s\), with separate weights for current-current and
left-right operators.
sections.post_2008:
The 2008 RS-flavor baseline in \texttt{cfw\_2008\_rs\_flavor} predates the
precision LHCb \(B_s\) oscillation program.  The key post-2008 experimental
step for this catalog is the LHCb measurement in
\texttt{lhcb\_2021\_deltams\_arxiv}: using \(6~{\rm fb}^{-1}\) of Run-2
\(B_s\to D_s\pi\) data, LHCb finds
\[
  \Delta m_s=17.7683\pm0.0051_{\rm stat}\pm0.0032_{\rm syst}~{\rm ps}^{-1},
\]
and a LHCb-only combination
\[
  \Delta m_s=17.7656\pm0.0057~{\rm ps}^{-1}.
\]
On the theory side, \texttt{flag\_2024\_bmixing} records the FLAG Review 2024
\(N_f=2+1+1\) auxiliary inputs
\[
  f_{B_s}\sqrt{\hat B_{B_s}}=256.1\pm5.7~{\rm MeV},\qquad
  \xi=1.216\pm0.016,
\]
with a warning that correlated decay-constant and mixing inputs should not be
reconstructed from separate averages.
sections.validity:
The experimental oscillation frequency is very clean.  The model-dependent step
is translating it into a new-physics allowance: a conservative catalog can bound
\(|M_{12}^{s,{\rm NP}}|\) directly from the measured splitting, while a sharper
SM-subtracted interpretation depends on CKM inputs and FLAG lattice matrix
elements.  The constraint is therefore a robust \(\Delta F=2\) magnitude test,
but cancellations between SM and new physics or correlated CKM-fit choices
should be made explicit in any live likelihood.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{YES-D2-BS.}  The v1 \(\Delta F=2\) input bundle defines the \(B_s\) system at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:238} with key \texttt{b\_s} at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:239} and down-sector generations
\texttt{(1, 2)} at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:244}.  The hadronic
constants include the \(B_s\) decay constant at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:645} and \(\Delta m_s\) input at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:651}.  The budget helper is at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:893}; the evaluator is
\texttt{evaluate\_bs\_mixing} at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:922},
with the QCD-running wrapper at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:969}.
The modern policy also carries \(B_s\) in
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23}, defines the policy entry
at \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:135}, and routes it through
the bridge evaluator at \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:657}
and \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:1346}.  The shared
\(\Delta F=2\) operator basis is documented at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/qcd\_running.py:7}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{LOW.}  The existing \(\Delta F=2\) basis and RG path already cover the
needed VLL, VRR, scalar, and left-right structures for \(B_s\) mixing.  Future
production work is mostly input hygiene: decide the HFLAV/PDG value policy,
refresh FLAG normalization constants, and document whether the live constraint
uses a conservative absolute \(M_{12}^{\rm NP}\) budget or an SM-subtracted one.
sections.key_references:
Process-local reference keys before bibliography consolidation:
\texttt{hflav\_2024\_dms}, \texttt{hflav\_pdg2025\_dms},
\texttt{lhcb\_2021\_deltams\_arxiv}, \texttt{flag\_2024\_bmixing}, and
\texttt{cfw\_2008\_rs\_flavor}.
